{{Quote|The last two Gaeriel Capens aud-stream hits, ''The Sequential Passage of Chronological Intervals'' and a remix of Dembaline's classic ''Shwock Dubllon'', are actually TribBot output.|HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|<ref name="hnn531">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|56|business/13516_4.html|Balmorran Secures Musician Rearmament Contract}}</ref>}}
'''Dembaline''' was a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Musician/Legends|composer]] whose most recognizable work, the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Shwock Dubllon]]," was produced prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] agency regarded "Shwock Dubllon" as a classic, and the song had been remixed by musician [[Gaeriel Capens]]. Dembaline and "Shwock Dubllon" retained their popularity across the span of several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]], and the piece was later used by a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], the [[Kyp's Dozen|Dozen-and-Two Avengers]], as its unofficial theme song.

==Biography==
[[File:Dac-AORCR.png|thumb|right|300px|One of Dembaline's songs, "Shwock Dubllon," was considered one of Dac's (pictured) significant contributions to galactic culture.]]
Dembaline was a member of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="vp" /> [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]].<ref name="tegtas">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> Dembaline became a [[Musician/Legends|composer]] at some point prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and produced a number of works that were notable in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], one of the more prominent being the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Shwock Dubllon]],"<ref name="vp" /> a [[Time/Legends|timeless]] masterpiece that served as most species' first exposure to Mon Calamari diaspora and was considered one of Dac's most significant contributions to [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] culture.<ref name="Slugthrowers">{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-an-overview-of-popular-music-and-musicians-in-a-galaxy-far-far-away-part-1|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> Translated into [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], "Shwock Dubllon" meant "Crested Wake," and it was known as a rousing, dramatic piece.<ref name="vp" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|56|business/13516_4.html|Balmorran Secures Musician Rearmament Contract}} is set on the date 13:5:16, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> the musician [[Gaeriel Capens]] released<ref name="hnn531" /> to renewed acclaim<ref name="Slugthrowers" /> a remix of "Shwock Dubllon." [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition]] later published about the remix, detailing that it was produced with the aid of [[RetSpan Audionics]] [[TribBot mimic series|TribBot mimic droids]].<ref name="hnn531" /> Dembaline's music remained popular following the Clone Wars and into the period of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]],<ref name="vp" /> which began in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> A [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] dedicated to tracking down [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], known as the [[Kyp's Dozen|Dozen-and-Two Avengers]] and led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyp Durron]],<ref name="vp" /> adopted "Shwock Dubllon" as its unofficial theme song.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|I|182|Dembaline}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
A musical composer of some renown, Dembaline's work retained its notability and popularity for many [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]. One of Dembaline's best known songs, "Shwock Dubllon," was regarded as a rousing and dramatic piece by some [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="vp" /> Dembaline was both a common name among [[Gender/Legends|female]] Mon Calamari and a type of [[Dembaline (gemstone)|homonymous gemstone]].<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Dembaline was first mentioned in the [[1999]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'', the first installment in [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series, which was written by [[R. A. Salvatore]].<ref name="vp" />
